item.content:encoded: It's over 900 now, onward to 1000!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This hurts, as my heart grows from the admirability for breaks from sadness. This is beautiful. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really, really good. Wholesome and sad. Unique and well done. Detailed enough to give you an idea of their world, but with enough room for imagination. This is among my favourites.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is very rare for me to become misty-eyed when reaching the end of an SCP, but that one achieved it. I already liked it but this was the final nail. Well done.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How did I not find this SCP before? My goodness, this is amazing. And the images from the Voyager just push it that much further. I genuinely hope that this is what alien life will be like, minus the whole species eradication part. Take my +1, you master of words, you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just wanted to add my sincere admiration of this article. The writing, the tone and especially the sense of positivity it leaves the reader with (even if if is tinged with sadness) all combine to make for a superb piece. Also those aliens are 100% The Elder Race from the mythos and I'm a little surprised noone mentioned it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That last sentence…wow. I think it's because I didn't see it coming, it really packed an emotional wallop. A really, unusually big one. I don't know why, but I felt my eyes beginning to moisten, and it's rare for a piece of fiction to do that to me. Superb writing!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is heart-wrenching and touching on a visceral level. This is unique, not just in the subject matter, but in the kinds of feelings it gets across. Hate to sound cheesy, but this, is nothing less than beautiful. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is… hard to describe in a few words.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no doubt that this will keep bringing all of us to tears for a lot longer than that. You're fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've read this multiple times and it still makes me want to fucking cry.

God, that last line hits so hard, right in the feels.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this. I love it with every fiber of my fucking being. Sure, yeah, maybe it's an emotionally manipulative piece that rides on the sense of sublimity that precludes contact, but fuck it, I really and truly love this.

My once criticism is the audio file. Maybe it's just cause I always imagined this playing during the last note, but it felt a bit… off kilter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was done with my blessing, I think it was much better than the one that came before it.

Just noting here in case any staff that do not know and might wanna revert.

Also, is it bad that I keep being brought to tears by things that I wrote myself even six years later? ;-;

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Audio file replaced.

Hardware:
AKG D5 Vocal Mic (added shock mount & pop filter)
Behringer U-Phoria UM2 Audio Interface

Software:
Audacity (recording & final mix)
Melodyne (editing)

Hope you all enjoy it!

item.title: Re: Proposed change to Audio File
item.content:encoded:
Yeah… That was me! I'm surprised I'm having people recognize me on here but I don't hate it

And yeah I sent him a private message about it

item.title: Re: Proposed change to Audio File
item.content:encoded:
I'd appreciate it if the author could take a look when possible

Have you sent a wikidot message to FlameShirt about this? If not, definitely do so. Alternatively, some community members who have additional means of communication can reach out.

Also… totally unrelated, but are you the same Flaminglog that created the Wii Honk Channel video? https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=A-5gAUF5inQ

If so, you're my hero. That video makes me so happy.

item.title: Proposed change to Audio File
item.content:encoded:
While the inclusion of the audio file is in itself a good idea, the file itself is somewhat poor quality. The voice acting itself is fine, but the effects feel kinda gaudy or tacked on, for lack of a better term.

Physiologically, we know that this species is 2 meters tall on average, and that it has three mouths.

This doesn't necessarily confirm the existence of three sets of vocal cords, but it certainly makes it likely. Additionally, the large size of the species (this is slightly larger than a human) would mean the voice should sound lower than that of a human, not higher.

So what I've put together is a proposed replacement for the file currently in the article. I'd appreciate it if the author could take a look when possible (I know the article is 6 years old, but it'll likely be up for a few more decades at least).

+ show block
– hide block

As a courtesy to our mobile readers, I've collapsed the sound html block. ~Zyn

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Someone said the foundation would be responsible for destroying a species to cover it up, but I prefer the Foundation as being morally grey. I think they would work towards acclimatizing humanity towards accepting the existence of alien life in order to prevent social disruption upon revelation, and preventing needless slaughter when they can.

If the Foundation is what seals the aliens fate, then it should only be for extreme reasons, like their world being overrun by Fifthism or something along those lines.

item.title: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded: Just an FYI, I went ahead and changed the audio player from Flash to HTML5, as Flash is rapidly losing support and already can't be viewed by many people, including Firefox and Mobile users. No content changes were made, and the article is otherwise unchanged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's what it was estimated to be, back in the 1970s.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: why does the voyager probe's image say pluto's diameter is 14000 km?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you, Flameshirt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
<FlameShirt>: it's because the writer is specifically referencing humans /before/ the damage they did
<weryllium>: "I really hope that you, /you/ (points to 2000s humanity), do"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Necroposting, but:

<FlameShirt>: Also, I'm really mad that people find the line "I really hope that you, you, do." in that scp really hard to scan

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"I really hope that you, you, do."

Should I fix this or am I just not seeing straight?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This…this hit home. I had to take a moment to calm down and stop crying before I finished this message, but this SCP's ending message is really important to me. I have deep-rooted depression issues and some self loathing on top of that, and something about that last sentence just hits me in the gut…but it sort of makes me happy at the same time.

I love this SCP. Thank you.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, there are ways to do vocal effects well, but this just…like, I couldn't NOT think of Ratboy Genius.

And while I unironically love Ratboy Genius, I don't really think that aesthetic fits well with this article, or with the Foundation as a whole.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The audio is really… "off". Like what others said, it sounded like something out of a cartoon, and it is pretty easy to tell that it is just a voice effect over normal human voices.

Context-wise, it doesn't add much to the article, and actually slightly detracts from the general theme of the article - the musical theme isn't carried well, and the effect ended up making the recording unintentionally hilarious whereas it should've evoked something deeper, like sympathy or wonder, instead of humor from slightly over-the-top effects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that the audio was really enjoyable. I listened to it while reading the text and even if it's a monotonous squeaky and robotic voice, it gave the note a kind of emotion that somehow made it better. I'm a fan of audio logs because of how rare they are. Generally when there's one, it fits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the inclusion of the audio file. I think it makes sense for it to be there, since the following document is a transcription of it, but I agree. I don't think it was done terribly well; it sounds like a cartoon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ow, my heart. It doesn't help that Voyager makes me feel harder than almost anything else, but then you just get this and… It's perfect. It's a great example of how SCPs don't have to just scare or be funny, they can be heartbreaking or thought-provoking, too. Fantastic article and definitely one of my favourites of all time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, it sounds like something from a cartoon. It seems to me a strange thing to include, it seems really out of place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Included an audio file by Cimmerian of the message.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These guys sound like pretentious hippies. No wonder we wiped them out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP reminds me his Right Honourable Carl Sagan who is my idol and short SF manga by Hoshino Yukinobu.

Human shoot an artifact into the cosmic ocean, but technology advance is far faster than that artifact's velocity, So before the artifact meet somebody else our humanity, humanity can contact that somebody else. In this situation, What is artifact worth?

Maybe nothing, everything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I only just today read this and I'm super-pissed that I never read it before. Add another +1 to your heaping pile, Author. You found all my heartstrings and soft spots and I am in love with this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "…hope that you, you, do." I don't like that second you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a note(since I've been asked). I asked for the author's permission to upload the file that I have uploaded. Edit: And got it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Missed an opportunity to have alien response

"SEND MORE CHUCK BERRY"
"SEND MORE CHUCK BERRY"
Happy to have given this article the upvote that pushed it to +300. Congrats!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wow. This is easily in the top 5 of any SCP I've ever read. Gut punch right in the feels. What hit me the most is that their final message was in vain, since the Foundation are the ones who got a hold of it. Nothing will change because no one will ever know.

I have many sad now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. I've read a lot of SCPs on this wiki ranging from absurd to thought provoking to horriffic, but this is the first one I've read that I could describe as touching. I almost shed a tear at the end of that last note. Well done and plus one

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Flameshirt uses FEELSPUNCH!
It's super effective :c +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
or to the reduce your impact upon our own species

Not quite sure what you were going for here so I'll leave it to someone else to correct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: end of the letter made me tear up. doesnt mean much since my tear ducts are faulty faucets, but +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neat feature I hadn't thought of, thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a thumbnail with the full-size picture available on click. Because cramming a 350Kb 2 258px × 2 788px picture into a 300px frame isn't really good. Revert if you will though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is my favourite SPC on the site besides 093 :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
good lord the feels.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was incredible. Fantastic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very, very well done. Thank you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Just re-read this. The last line was as chilling, haunting, and beautiful as it was the first time. However, when reading the containment procedures, I came across this:

As SCP-1342-3 is unlikely to be containable in the near future, Project Heimdall is to continue in its Contingency Planning Operation.

And got chills all over again, but of a totally different kind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well for one thing, divergent realities do exist in this world, or at least, they do if you accept certain SCPs as canon. This is, after all, anomalous materials; why shouldn't they turn time into a pretzel or worse? And there's no evidence the Foundation did this; they tend to form mutually beneficial relations with cooperative governments and only hide them because they're anomalous. Presumably, this is something humanity did in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…I'm not sure if time works that way. It depends on if you include divergent realities or not. It's quite possible that by sending this voyager imitation back in time they caused a divergent reality. Their original reality's people is doomed to extinction by our hand but their decision causes another reality's to live on in peace with the humans.
Also I'm pretty sure the Foundation wouldn't be big enough dipshits to stomp them in the face. It's "secure contain protect" not "destroy destroy destroy". Or maybe humanity fails in the future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just read this for, I think, the third time. That note at the end still makes tears well up in my eyes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: such beautiful words. a worthy goodbye, for such a civilization.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I seriously got chills reading the message. Imagine how groundbreaking it would be if voyager returned, or if we received radio messages. How great that would be!

I can only hope, though I doubt it will come in my lifetime let alone at all. *Sigh*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't feel fear, but sadness. What a tragedy that this had to happen. I wonder if the message was never sent at all.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. Foundation finds SCP-1342-1 and -2
  2. At some point between now and 40,000 years hence, communication opens between humans and SCP-1342-3.
  3. SCP-1342-3 tech levels approach humanity's, due to inception of unsecured radio transmissions.
  4. Humans and SCP-1342-3 manage to collaboratively build a FTL device which, because of relativity, can essentially transport something anywhere and to any time.
  5. Humanity stomps SCP-1342-3 in the face. Who wants the competition?
  6. SCP-1342-3 find Voyager and sends SCP-1342-1 and -2 in response, through a barely functional FTL device.
  7. Go back to step 1.

If the SCP-1342-3 isn't attacked, we wouldn't have SCP-1342-1 and -2 in containment.
